name: NIST SP 800-172
fullName: Enhanced Security Requirements for Controlled Unclassified Information
description: Advanced security practices engineered to stop Advanced Persistent Threats (APTs) targeting high-value defense data.

questions.name: NIST SP 800-172 - Advanced Authentication
questions.text: Do system accesses utilize hardware trust anchors (e.g. FIDO2 tokens) (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:
Audit active MFA setups, hardware token distribution logs, and gate parameters.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the enhanced access control configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Unauthenticated or unmonitored IT-OT bridge endpoints can expose critical NIST 800 172 systems to lateral network pivoting. An administrative compromise in the enterprise domain (such as phishing or AD account compromise) can lead directly to unauthorized SCADA control commands.

questions.name: NIST SP 800-172 - Visual Session Recording
questions.text: Are active administrative remote sessions visually recorded and reviewed (utilizing secure Jump Hosts, MFA validation nodes, active directory GPOs, and hardware tokens)?
questions.description:

questions.name: NIST SP 800-172 - Product Verification Scanning
questions.text: Are newly purchased hardware modules scanned for physical supply chain tampering?
questions.description:
Verify physical inspection registers, verify chassis seal numbers, and check chip codes.

SOP:
1. Enforce strict role-based access controls (RBAC) separating administrative tasks from standard operator routines.
2. Route all incoming remote connections through isolated administrative Jump Hosts with visual session logging active.
3. Conduct quarterly access audits to identify and completely disable dormant or inactive accounts.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the supply chain risk management configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: NIST SP 800-172 - Physical Substations CCTV Vetting
questions.text: Are critical enclaves protected by biometric entry gates and physical perimeters?
questions.description:
Verify physical lock gates, check badge-reader entry logs, and review camera feeds.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the physical security strategy configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.

questions.name: NIST SP 800-172 - Disaster Recovery Failover Plan Strategy
questions.text: Is an active energy infrastructure disaster recovery failover plan tested annually?
questions.description:
Review failover test logs, check backup server registers, and inspect procedures.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the operations continuity strategy configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: General OT/IT security evidence must include: change management tracking tickets, Active Directory Group Policy Objects (GPOs), system log archives, and Nozomi/Dragos anomaly monitoring configuration files.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.name: NIST SP 800-172 - Backup Isolation Checks Strategy

questions.description:

SOP:
